title: 'Abigail' Global Box Office Sinks Its Teeth Into a Record
text: The horror comedy film Abigail achieved a strong opening weekend, earning $15.2 million globally. The movie, directed by Radio Silence's Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett, made $10.2 million domestically, while internationally the film made just over $5 million. Abigail is a vampire horror film featuring an ensemble cast including Melissa Barrera, Dan Stevens, Kathryn Newton, Angus Cloud, William Catlett, Giancarlo Esposito, and Alisha Weir. The story follows criminals who kidnap what they believe is an ordinary girl, only to discover they kidnapped not only a vampire but Dracula's twisted daughter. The film performed well considering its modest budget. It opened across 62 international markets on over 7,000 screens, with Mexico leading international numbers. The opening compares favorably to similar horror films, positioning itself as a solid debut for the genre. The film earned positive reviews, becoming certified fresh on Rotten Tomatoes with an 83% critic score. Industry observers expect the movie to maintain momentum at the box office in coming weeks based on strong word-of-mouth reception.
